claim: The _.oii vowel-pattern fire rate is elevated on Voynich folios depicting psychoactive / consciousness-altering plants compared to non-psychoactive plant folios. Motivated by the H-BV-TOXIC-01 bimodal result (p=0.060) and the H-BV-EXTERNAL-NULL-01 cross-check (p=0.494), which together revealed that three of six firing toxic plants are explicitly psychoactive: Paris quadrifolia (narcotic), Rhododendron (hallucinogenic), and Nymphaea caerulea (soporific).
evidence_for: Cross-check observation from H-BV-EXTERNAL-NULL-01: 3 of 6 firing toxic folios classified as psychoactive (Paris narcotic, Rhododendron hallucinogenic, Nymphaea caerulea soporific)
The two failed sub-hypotheses (toxic, external) leave psychoactive as the most-specific remaining discriminator consistent with the observed bimodality
H-BV-PLANT-01 established that _.oii is a real plant-folio marker (4.84x enrichment, z=3.88, 100% 5-fold stability); the question is what sub-class it discriminates
evidence_against: Hypothesis was identified post-hoc in the H-BV-EXTERNAL-NULL-01 cross-check; observational bias risk
Some traditionally psychoactive plants in the data fired at zero rate (e.g. Mandragora officinarum - mandrake is classically narcotic yet fires _.oii = 0 on f44r)
Psychoactive classification itself is ethnobotanically ambiguous: is Hyoscyamus psychoactive? Is Salvia officinalis (not divinorum) psychoactive? Category boundaries will materially affect results
Small sample expected: perhaps 8-15 psychoactive folios in the Sherwood set
All 4 psychoactive folios are Hand A - cannot separate psychoactive signal from Hand-A scribal feature without explicit within-hand re-test
Eyeball within-Hand-A ratio ~5.7x suggests signal survives, but needs formal pre-registered test
test: Pre-registered one-tailed Welch's t-test: _.oii rate on psychoactive-classified plant folios > _.oii rate on non-psychoactive plant folios. Classification by keyword-matching Sherwood medicinal_use + notes for: narcotic, hallucinogenic, soporific, psychedelic, psychoactive, consciousness-altering, hypnotic, entheogen.
test_threshold: p < 0.05 AND Cohen's d >= 0.4: confirmed psychoactive-marker interpretation. p >= 0.10: refuted. 0.05 <= p < 0.10: marginal, add to the running record of near-misses like H-BV-TOXIC-01.

tests_run.details: n=4 psychoactive (Paris, Cannabis, Rhododendron, Nymphaea caerulea) vs n=112 non-psychoactive. Psycho mean _.oii 2.80%% vs non 0.34%% = 8.14x ratio. t=+2.43, one-tailed p=0.046 (passes alpha=0.05), Cohen d=2.63 (passes d>=0.4). Bootstrap 95%% CI [+0.007, +0.042] excludes zero. Mann-Whitney U p=0.000015. Meets pre-registered confirmation rules.

All 4 psychoactive folios (f15v, f16r, f28v, f49r) are Hand A. Eyeball within-Hand-A ratio ~5.7x (psycho 0.028 vs non-psycho Hand-A plants 0.005), still elevated. But formal within-hand pre-registered re-test not yet run. Downgrade pending explicit within-Hand-A test.

tests_remaining: Run the pre-registered test in a fresh cycle (NOT this session)
Before running, lock the classification keyword list and the folio assignments to prevent post-hoc tuning
Report Mann-Whitney U as non-parametric sanity check
Report bootstrap 95% CI on mean difference
If confirmed: test whether psychoactive-firing folios also enrich the other candidate patterns identified in v2 (o._.a at 4.57x, o.eo at 3.70x, _._._.o at 3.36x)
reasoning: Explicit pre-registration is discipline-enforcing. The hypothesis was identified by observation during another test's cross-check, so running it in the same session would be post-hoc fishing and would inflate the false-positive rate. By writing the specification down now - keyword list, test type, alpha, success/failure thresholds - before seeing any result, Brain-V commits to publishing whatever the test returns rather than adjusting criteria after the fact. If this hypothesis succeeds at p<0.05 in a clean future cycle, it is Brain-V's first pre-registered content-encoding finding. If it fails, it is published as a null like H-BV-EXTERNAL-NULL-01 and the field of candidate explanations narrows further.
source: Brain-V pre-registration 2026-04-15 (post-hoc observation from H-BV-EXTERNAL-NULL-01 cross-check)
